name: Pliego Castle and Alto del Cairel Trail
name_original: Pliego, Ermita De Los Remedios, Castillo, Cruz de Pliego – Ruta Circular
description: This easy 6.3-mile mountain bike trail near Pliego offers 927 feet of elevation gain and takes about 1 hour to complete, featuring historic
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Pliego traverse a varied landscape, characterized by river valleys, forested areas, and historical landmarks. The region offers routes that explore natural features like reservoirs and waterfalls, alongside paths that lead past ancient structures. Mountain biking here includes ascents to elevated viewpoints and descents through natural terrain. Best mountain bike trails around Pliego The most popular mountain bike trail is Pliego Castle and Alto del Cairel Trail, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 3 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the historic castle and surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Luz Reservoir – Mula River Viaduct loop from Mula, a moderate 23.2 miles (37.4 km) path. This route features scenic water bodies and impressive engineering structures. Local mountain bikers also love the Trail through pine trees loop from Pliego, a 29.9 miles (48.2 km) trail leading through dense pine forests, often completed in about 3 hours 45 minutes. Mountain biking around Pliego is defined by pine forests, river valleys, and historical sites like castles. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Pliego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Pliego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Pliego?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails in the Pliego region, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and challenging routes to explore.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Pliego offers several easy mountain bike trails. A great option for beginners or families is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936911407/pliego-castle-and-alto-del-cairel-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pliego Castle and Alto del Cairel Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 10 km long and provides scenic views without being overly strenuous.

Q: What are the options for more advanced mountain bikers in Pliego?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Pliego has 17 difficult routes. These trails often feature significant ascents and technical descents through varied terrain, including pine forests and river valleys. While specific advanced routes are not detailed here, you can find them within the komoot guide.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that feature waterfalls or water bodies?
A: Yes, you can find routes that incorporate water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33799943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Usero Waterfall – Salto del Usero loop from Mula&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you past the beautiful Usero Waterfall. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31404178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Luz Reservoir – Mula River Viaduct loop from Mula&lt;/a&gt;, which features scenic water bodies.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in the Pliego area?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails around Pliego are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31404178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Luz Reservoir – Mula River Viaduct loop from Mula&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14626712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail through pine trees loop from Pliego&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the trails?
A: The trails around Pliego offer a mix of natural beauty and historical sites. You might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5533456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mula River Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2651129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Caputa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or ride through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4861055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Espuña North-facing Slope&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites like the Old Baños de Mula Railway Station are also accessible.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Pliego?
A: The mountain bike trails in Pliego are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the varied landscape, which includes river valleys, dense pine forests, and historical landmarks, offering a diverse and engaging experience.

Q: Are there any specific trailheads or starting points for mountain biking in Pliego?
A: Many routes originate directly from Pliego itself or nearby towns like Mula. Specific trailheads are often indicated on individual route pages on komoot, providing details on where to begin your ride. Parking is generally available in these starting areas, though it's always good to check local signage.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Pliego?
A: The region around Pliego, being in Murcia, Spain, generally offers good conditions for mountain biking outside of the peak summer months when temperatures can be very high. Spring and autumn are ideal, with milder weather and pleasant conditions for exploring the trails. Winter can also be suitable, though some higher elevation areas might be cooler.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking on trails around Pliego?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on the established trails around Pliego. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations, especially when riding through protected natural areas like the Barrancos de Gebas Protected Landscape, and to stay on marked paths.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete in Pliego?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936911407/pliego-castle-and-alto-del-cairel-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pliego Castle and Alto del Cairel Trail&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour. Moderate to difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14626712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail through pine trees loop from Pliego&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 3 hours 45 minutes or more, depending on your pace and stops.
tips: Charming hermitage in baroque style that houses the patron saint of the town. The original structure of the hermitage has been remodeled. There is not much information about the original, but it is to be assumed that its foundation was in the 16th century.
The castle was built as a defensive enclave and maintained this function until the 16th century when it was abandoned. It has been partially restored recently.
From the "Mirador de la Cruz" you can see magnificent views of all of Pliego.

Rolling along the highway of the Murcia region (RM-515) with some sections of asphalt in poor condition but which will be completely rehabilitated (in theory) during 2022. We will cross this hill at 465 meters of altitude which bears the name of the beautiful Sierra that will give us beautiful pictures along this road.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Pliego Castle and Alto del Cairel Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter a steep slab-covered road leading up to the Castle of Pliego, followed by a dirt path. While generally well-marked, some sections in the broader region can be technical, with loose boulders or chunky terrain, so be prepared for varied conditions.

faqs.question: How difficult is this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this trail as 'easy' for mountain biking, with an elevation gain of approximately 283 meters over its 10.2 km distance. However, be aware that the route includes a steep, slab-covered road, and mountain biking in Southern Spain can often involve technical sections. While the main route is well-marked, always assess conditions and your skill level, especially if exploring off the main path.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Pliego Castle and Alto del Cairel Trail?
faqs.answer: This route offers a fantastic blend of history and natural beauty. You'll pass by the historic Castle of Pliego, the charming Ermita de Nuestra Señora de los Remedios, and the Mirador de la Cruz de Pliego. The trail also provides magnificent views of Sierra Espuña, Pliego, and Mula, with several viewpoints offering expansive panoramas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details are not provided, but as the trail is a circular route starting and ending near Pliego, it's generally recommended to look for parking within or on the outskirts of the town of Pliego itself. From there, you can easily access the start of the route.

faqs.question: Is the Pliego Castle and Alto del Cairel Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it is located within the Sierra Espuña Regional Park, it's important to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the Sierra Espuña Regional Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required to access the Pliego Castle and Alto del Cairel Trail or the general areas of the Sierra Espuña Regional Park for recreational activities like mountain biking. However, always respect local regulations and signage.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Murcia, Southern Spain, the best times to mountain bike this trail are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler with occasional rain.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Pliego Castle and Alto del Cairel Trail (PR-MU77) shares sections with other official routes. You'll find it overlaps with parts of the 'Castillo de Pliego y Alto del Cairel' route, and also briefly with 'Sendero de La Almoloya de Pliego' and 'Senda Sierra Manzanete'.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is located near the town of Pliego, which offers various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in Pliego to refuel and relax before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Even though it's rated 'easy', it's always wise to be prepared. Bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months, snacks, a basic bike repair kit, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, and sun protection. A fully charged phone with the Komoot app for navigation is also recommended, as the trails are well-marked but having a map is always a good idea.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Pliego Castle and Alto del Cairel Trail is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area, allowing you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps.
